chunk_summary: This section discusses CMAM program performance indicators and Sphere benchmarks for managing severe acute malnutrition (SAM), focusing on discharge follow-up, program quality, and global guidelines for community-based interventions.
learning_objectives: Understand the Sphere benchmarks for CMAM program performance in SAM management.
Identify key indicators for evaluating the quality of discharge follow-up in SAM programs.
Analyze global guidelines and recommendations for community-based management of SAM.
Explore the role of international organizations in setting standards for SAM interventions.
key_facts: Sphere benchmarks recommend a cure rate of >75%, a death rate of <10%, and a default rate of <15% for SAM programs.
The World Health Organization emphasizes community-based management as a critical approach to addressing SAM.
The NITI Aayog report highlights India's efforts in implementing CMAM programs to reduce malnutrition rates.
Joint guidelines by WHO, WFP, UNSCN, and UNICEF (2007) stress the importance of integrating SAM management into primary healthcare systems.
Discharge follow-up is essential to ensure sustained recovery and prevent relapse in SAM cases.
reference_quote: "Sphere standards recommend a cure rate of >75%, a death rate of <10%, and a default rate of <15% for SAM programs." (Source: Sphere Project Handbook, 2011)
document_key_topics: Global guidelines for community-based SAM interventions
